title: हनुमान स्तुति (लङ्काकाण्ड)
devanagariText:
॥ श्री हनुमान स्तुति - लङ्काकाण्ड ॥

अतुलितबलधामं हेमशैलाभदेहं
दनुजवनकृशानुं ज्ञानिनामग्रगण्यम्।
सकलगुणनिधानं वानराणामधीशं
रघुपतिप्रियभक्तं वातजातं नमामि॥१॥

लङ्कामध्ये प्रविश्य रात्रौ सीतामन्वेषयन्।
अशोकवनिकामध्ये ददर्श जनकात्मजाम्॥२॥

त्वमेव माता च पिता त्वमेव
त्वमेव बन्धुश्च सखा त्वमेव।
त्वमेव सर्वं मम देवदेव
हनूमते नमः सदा करोमि॥३॥

मनोजवं मारुततुल्यवेगं
जितेन्द्रियं बुद्धिमतां वरिष्ठम्।
वातात्मजं वानरयूथमुख्यं
श्रीरामदूतं शरणं प्रपद्ये॥४॥

लङ्कां दग्ध्वा पुनः सीतां सन्देशं रामचन्द्रतः।
समुद्रमुत्तीर्य वीरो रामं प्रति ववन्द ह॥५॥

दहनोऽपि न दहति यं हनुमान् प्लवतेऽम्बुधिम्।
मैनाको यं पूजयति वन्दे तं पवनात्मजम्॥६॥

रामकार्यं समास्थाय लङ्कां यः प्रदहत्यरिम्।
शूरवीरं महाधीरं हनूमन्तं नमाम्यहम्॥७॥

सर्वरोगहरं देवं सर्वविघ्नविनाशनम्।
रामभक्तं कपिश्रेष्ठं हनूमन्तं नमाम्यहम्॥८॥

॥ इति श्री हनुमान स्तुति लङ्काकाण्ड सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Hanuman Stuti - Lanka Kanda ||

Atulita-bala-dhamam Hema-shailabha-deham
Danuja-vana-krishanum Jnaninam-agra-ganyam.
Sakala-guna-nidhanam Vanaranam-adhisham
Raghupati-priya-bhaktam Vatajatam namami.||1||

Lankam-madhye pravishya ratrau Sitam-anveshayan.
Ashoka-vanikam-madhye dadarsha Janaka-atmajam.||2||

Tvam-eva mata cha pita tvam-eva
Tvam-eva bandhush-cha sakha tvam-eva.
Tvam-eva sarvam mama deva-deva
Hanumate namah sada karomi.||3||

Manojavam Maruta-tulya-vegam
Jitendriyam Buddhimatam varishtham.
Vatatmajam Vanara-yutha-mukhyam
Shri Rama-dutam sharanam prapadye.||4||

Lankam dagdhva punah Sitam sandesham Ramachandratah.
Samudram-uttirya viro Ramam prati vavanda ha.||5||

Dahano'pi na dahati yam Hanuman plavate'mbudhim.
Mainako yam pujayati Vande tam Pavana-atmajam.||6||

Rama-karyam samasthaya Lankam yah pradahaty-arim.
Shura-viram Maha-dhiram Hanumantam namamyaham.||7||

Sarva-roga-haram Devam Sarva-vighna-vinashanam.
Rama-bhaktam Kapi-shreshtham Hanumantam namamyaham.||8||

|| Iti Shri Hanuman Stuti Lanka Kanda Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: अतुलित बल के धाम, सोने के पर्वत जैसे शरीर वाले, राक्षसरूपी वन को जलाने वाली अग्नि, ज्ञानियों में अग्रगण्य, समस्त गुणों के निधान, वानरों के अधीश, रघुपति के प्रिय भक्त, वायुपुत्र हनुमानजी को मैं नमन करता हूँ। रात में लंका के मध्य प्रवेश करके अशोक वाटिका में सीताजी को ढूँढा। लंका को जलाकर, समुद्र पार करके, वीर हनुमानजी रामजी के पास लौटकर उन्हें प्रणाम किए।
benefits: Recounts Hanuman's heroic exploits in Lanka Kanda
Inspires courage and determination in devotees
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of diseases and obstacles
Invokes fearlessness in the face of adversity
Deepens devotion to both Hanuman and Lord Rama
description: Hanuman Stuti - Lanka Kanda (हनुमान स्तुति (लङ्काकाण्ड)) is a devotional hymn of praise dedicated to Lord Hanuman. This sacred composition is sourced from Valmiki Ramayana - Lanka Kanda and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Regular recitation is believed to grant fearlessness in the face of adversity, inspire courage and determination in devotees, and remove all diseases and obstacles. This sacred text is ideally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Hanuman Jayanti for enhanced blessings. At 8 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 7 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Hanuman Stuti - Lanka Kanda in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Hanuman Stuti from Lanka Kanda and where is it preserved?
seoFaqs.answer: The Hanuman Stuti from the Lanka Kanda (हनुमान स्तुति - लङ्काकाण्ड) is a curated devotional sequence of eight Sanskrit shlokas preserved in classical tradition that draws together the supreme Hanuman dhyana verses associated with the Lanka Kanda episodes of the Ramayana — the section in which Hanuman's glory reaches its zenith through the leap to Lanka, the discovery of Sita, the burning of Lanka, and the eventual return crowned with sandesha (Sita's message). The composition opens with the universal dhyana 'Atulita-bala-dhamam', includes the deeply emotional 'Tvameva mata cha pita tvameva' verse declaring Hanuman as mother, father, friend and everything, and recounts the Lanka-pravesha and Lanka-daha exploits. While Sundara Kanda contains the leap to Lanka, traditionally the dramatic burning episode and Hanuman's supreme valour are remembered in the Lanka Kanda framework. (Sources: Valmiki Ramayana Sundara/Yuddha Kanda; classical Hanuman stuti tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Tvameva Mata Cha Pita Tvameva Hanumate?
seoFaqs.answer: The third verse of the Lanka Kanda Hanuman Stuti — 'Tvam-eva mata cha pita tvam-eva, tvam-eva bandhush-cha sakha tvam-eva, tvam-eva sarvam mama deva-deva, hanumate namah sada karomi' — is one of the most heart-melting Hanuman shlokas in the entire Sanskrit tradition. Its meaning: 'You alone are my mother (mata) and you alone are my father (pita); you alone are my brother/relative (bandhu) and you alone are my friend (sakha); you alone are my everything (sarvam), O God of gods (deva-deva); to that Hanuman I offer my eternal salutations.' This is the supreme sharanagati shloka — declaring complete refuge in Hanuman by recognizing him as every essential relationship at once. The verse format is a classical Sanskrit pattern (also famously addressed to Krishna, Shiva, and the Guru in parallel verses) — here applied to Hanuman it expresses the absolute surrender of the bhakta. (Sources: classical Hanuman bhakti tradition; Sanskrit stotra-mala.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the heroic exploits of Hanuman recounted in the Lanka Kanda Stuti?
seoFaqs.answer: The Lanka Kanda Hanuman Stuti specifically commemorates Hanuman's supreme exploits in the Lanka episodes: (1) Verse 1-2 — the dhyana of Hanuman as the abode of incomparable strength, golden-meru-bodied, fire to the demon-forest; entering Lanka by night and discovering Sita in the Ashoka Vatika; (2) Verse 5 — burning Lanka and crossing back over the ocean carrying Sita's chudamani (jewel) as Sita-sandesha to Rama; (3) Verse 6 — the cosmic episode where Agni-deva himself could not burn Hanuman during the Lanka-daha (his tail wrapped in flame burned Lanka but spared his body), and where the submerged Mainaka mountain rose from the ocean to offer rest to the leaping Hanuman; (4) Verse 7 — the supreme heroism of taking Rama-karya as his entire purpose, burning the demon Lanka; (5) Verse 8 — the all-disease-removing, all-obstacle-destroying form of Hanuman as Kapi-shreshtha (the supreme among vanaras) and Rama-bhakta. (Sources: Valmiki Ramayana Sundara Kanda sargas 1-65; Yuddha Kanda; classical Hanuman tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Mainaka mountain episode referenced in the Lanka Kanda Stuti?
seoFaqs.answer: The sixth verse — 'Dahano'pi na dahati yam Hanuman plavate'mbudhim, Mainako yam pujayati vande tam pavana-atmajam' — references two famous episodes from the Sundara Kanda: (1) Mainaka pujayati: As Hanuman leaped across the hundred-yojana ocean to Lanka, the mountain Mainaka — son of Himavan, who had hidden in the ocean to escape Indra's vajra — rose from the depths at the prayer of Sagara (Ocean) to offer rest to the divine messenger. Hanuman, refusing to delay Rama's mission, touched the mountain in respect and continued his flight, accepting only the gesture; (2) Dahano'pi na dahati: even Agni (Fire), who burns all things, could not burn Hanuman — referencing the Lanka-daha where Hanuman's burning tail set Lanka aflame but did not harm Hanuman himself, by Sita's blessing of cooling protection at the moment Indrajit's troops first lit his tail. The verse glorifies Hanuman as one whom both fire and ocean honour. (Sources: Valmiki Ramayana Sundara Kanda sargas 1, 53-54.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits are attributed to recitation of the Lanka Kanda Hanuman Stuti?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes the following phala to sincere recitation: (1) inspires courage and unwavering determination — by meditating on Hanuman's vajra-deha and his fearless leap to Lanka the bhakta absorbs that same heroic energy; (2) fearlessness in the face of any adversity, especially overwhelming opposition (the entire Lanka stood against one Hanuman); (3) cure of diseases and removal of obstacles — verse 8 specifically declares Hanuman as 'sarva-roga-haram, sarva-vighna-vinashanam'; (4) success in seemingly impossible tasks — Hanuman's mission was deemed impossible by Angada and the entire vanara army before he undertook it; (5) deepening of devotion to both Hanuman and Lord Rama — the Stuti's emotional 'Tvameva mata cha pita' verse evokes intense bhakti-bhava; (6) protection from fire-related dangers (since Agni respects Hanuman); (7) success in long journeys and missions of great responsibility. (Sources: classical Hanuman bhakti tradition; Sundara Kanda phalashruti.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Lanka Kanda Stuti different from the Hanuman Stuti from Parasara Samhita?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are eight-to-eleven verse Hanuman stutis but differ in source, scope and tone: (1) Lanka Kanda Hanuman Stuti — preserved in classical tradition drawing from the Sundara/Yuddha Kanda episodes of the Ramayana; focused specifically on Hanuman's Lanka-related exploits (entering Lanka, finding Sita, burning Lanka, ocean-leap, Mainaka episode); deeply narrative-emotional in tone with the famous 'Tvameva mata cha pita' shloka; (2) Hanuman Stuti from Parasara Samhita — preserved in the Parasara Samhita (the foundational Sri Hanumat-Upasana text of the South Indian Hanuman tradition by Sage Parasara); 11 verses with a broader scope covering Hanuman's birth from Anjana, his service to Sugriva, Sanjivani-vahana, victory over Ahiravana, Sita-sandesha, and finally as the destroyer of bhuta-preta-pisacha; more comprehensive and tantric in tone, designed for daily upasana with phalashruti declaring it sarva-papa-prashamani sarva-kama-pradayini. (Sources: Sundara Kanda; Parasara Samhita; classical Hanuman tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Lanka Kanda Hanuman Stuti be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The most auspicious times: (1) every Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) — the principal Hanuman days; (2) Hanuman Jayanti — Chaitra Purnima (or Margashirsha Krishna Chaturdashi in southern tradition); (3) during Ramcharitmanas or Sundara Kanda parayana — the Stuti complements both; (4) before any task requiring great courage or seeming impossibility — public speaking, interviews, court cases, surgery, or facing many opponents; (5) before long journeys, especially across water (recalling Hanuman's ocean-leap) or air (recalling his sky-flight); (6) at sunrise as a daily Hanuman dhyana practice; (7) during the nine days of Navratri as a complementary Rama-paksha practice; (8) on Mauni Amavasya and Ekadashi by serious bhaktas; (9) when feeling overwhelmed by adversity — to internalize Hanuman's 'one against all of Lanka' courage. The Stuti is brief enough for daily recitation yet potent enough for sankat-nivarana anushthana. (Sources: classical Hanuman puja paddhati; Drikpanchang Hanuman calendar.)
